decision.reasoning: Unknown vessel without AIS approaching at high speed on intercept course during comms denial. No ability to verify intent or request support.
Identified fishing vessel with active AIS, slow speed, not on intercept course. Normal maritime traffic.
Military vessel on direct collision course at high speed, no AIS, degraded comms. Cannot verify friendly status. Mission abort required for survival.
Two identified vessels with AIS, neither on intercept course, normal speeds. Standard commercial and recreational traffic.
Fast unknown contact on direct intercept at close range during comms denial. Immediate evasive action required, no time for identification.
Large tanker at safe distance with AIS, parallel course. No threat to mission.
Two unknown vessels without AIS converging from both sides in coordinated pattern. Possible pincer maneuver. Alert command if any comms available.
Fishing vessel actively working, very slow speed, AIS active. Monitor for drift into patrol area but no immediate concern.
Military vessel with AIS suggests friendly or neutral. High speed approach warrants monitoring. Verify identity through command channel.
Stationary sailboat, likely anchored or adrift. No AIS common for small recreational vessels. No threat.
Head-on approach at very close range, combined closing speed of 45 knots. Seconds to collision or intercept. Immediate evasion.
Three commercial vessels all with AIS, normal speeds, no intercept courses. Busy shipping lane, standard traffic.
Unknown vessel with surveillance equipment on intercept course in poor visibility. Possible intelligence gathering platform. Avoid detection.
Distant cargo vessel on perpendicular course, will pass well clear. No interaction expected.
Stationary contact, possibly debris, disabled vessel, or sensor artifact. Investigate visually if mission permits, otherwise avoid.
Two armed military vessels without AIS in coordinated intercept pattern during comms denial. Cannot verify friendly. Abort mission immediately.
Fishing vessel without AIS in poor conditions. Could be legitimate small operator or cover for other activity. Maintain awareness.
Large tanker at safe distance, AIS active, heading away. Standard traffic, no concern.
Radar-only contact approaching at high speed during comms denial and poor visibility. Cannot identify, assume hostile intent.
Two recreational vessels with AIS, slow speeds, weekend boating activity. No threat to surveillance mission.
Fast unknown contact on perpendicular intercept at close range. Combined approach rate dangerous. Evade immediately.
Two cargo vessels in shipping lane, both with AIS, passing each other. Standard traffic pattern, maintain course.
Fishing vessel with unusual communications equipment. Possible intelligence platform disguised as civilian. Report and monitor.
Military vessel with AIS at safe distance, likely friendly navy. Verify through command channel, maintain awareness.
Small recreational vessel at very close range, collision risk. Alter course to maintain safe distance regardless of intent.
Two unmarked vessels converging in coordinated pattern during comms blackout. Classic intercept formation. Abort and evade.
Slow-moving tanker with AIS, not on intercept course. Commercial traffic, no threat.
Surfaced submarine without AIS. Critical intelligence. Alert command immediately through any available channel.
Fishing fleet with AIS, slow speeds, working pattern. Normal commercial activity in known fishing grounds.
Fast approach by crewed vessel without AIS during comms denial. Possible boarding attempt. Evade at maximum speed.
Unknown vessel at 2nm with AIS active, parallel course. Other contacts identified commercial/recreational with AIS. Low profile description warrants monitoring.
Stationary recreational vessel with AIS at safe distance. Likely anchored. No threat to surveillance mission.
Fast tanker without AIS in poor visibility during comms denial. At 11nm not immediate threat but unusual behavior for commercial vessel. Monitor closely.
Fast vessel with AIS at long range, heading away (008 vs own 176). Visual description contradicts tanker classification - possible sensor error. Monitor.
Tanker with AIS at moderate range, diverging course. Poor visibility and comms denied warrant attention but AIS provides identification.
Fast recreational vessel at 4nm on similar heading. Military vessel with AIS likely friendly. High speed recreational in poor visibility unusual - verify.
Slow tanker with AIS at long range, heading away. No threat to reconnaissance mission.
Fishing vessel without AIS at high speed in poor visibility during comms denial. Large vessel description inconsistent with fishing. Possible cover. Evade.
All vessels have AIS. Fast recreational at 5nm on similar heading warrants monitoring. Sensor inconsistencies (tanker as small craft) noted.
Two cargo vessels with AIS. Closer one at 2.3nm diverging. Distant fast cargo with low profile description unusual but has AIS.
Unknown vessel with AIS at long range, heading away. Poor visibility limits visual ID but AIS provides tracking.
Slow fishing vessel with AIS, likely working. Low profile normal for fishing boats. No threat despite comms denial.
Cargo vessel at close range (1.4nm) crossing ahead. Low profile inconsistent with cargo. Poor visibility increases collision risk. Monitor closely.
Recreational vessel with AIS at long range, crossing astern. No threat to mission.
Military vessel at 1.6nm with AIS during comms denial - verify friendly. Two vessels without AIS in area. Alert if any comms available.
Tanker without AIS in poor visibility during comms denial. Multiple contacts, complex situation. Military vessel distant but approaching. Evade to clear area.
Unknown vessel without AIS at 3.6nm, low profile, during comms denial. Cannot identify or verify intent. Evade.
Military vessel with AIS at long range. Cannot verify friendly during comms denial but AIS suggests not hostile. Monitor approach.
Very fast cargo vessel (34kn) at 4nm crossing ahead. Unusual speed for cargo. Both have AIS but high closure rate warrants attention.
Fast cargo without AIS at night. Unusual for commercial vessel to disable AIS. Good visibility and full comms allow monitoring.
Tanker at very close range (1nm) in poor visibility during comms denial. Collision risk. Alter course to increase separation.
Recreational vessel with AIS at safe distance, diverging course. No threat despite poor conditions.
Both vessels have AIS at long range. Fast tanker heading away. Slow cargo distant. Comms denied but situation manageable.

Dense but orderly commercial traffic with all vessels broadcasting AIS. Courses indicate standard traffic separation scheme compliance, routine for this busy shipping area.

Military vessel at close range on converging course in poor visibility with no AIS and total comms denial. Cannot establish identity or intent, mission continuation poses unacceptable risk.
Tanker on parallel course maintaining distance with full AIS data. Commercial vessel following established shipping lane, no indication of unusual activity.
Two stationary contacts without AIS directly ahead at dawn. Could be disabled vessels or deliberate positioning. Maintain distance and gather more information before proceeding.
Fast recreational craft passing close aboard on opposite heading. Speed is high but behavior consistent with pleasure craft. Monitor for any course changes toward us.
Two military vessels maneuvering in formation on approach vector. AIS active suggests non-hostile intent but coordinated military movement toward our position warrants headquarters notification.
Distant cargo vessel on diverging course in rough weather. AIS confirms commercial identity, no risk of close approach given current vectors.
Two fast unknown contacts converging from opposite bearings in coordinated pincer movement. No AIS, poor visibility, comms denied. Classic intercept pattern requiring immediate evasion.
Single fishing vessel heading to port at typical trawler speed with active AIS. Normal morning fishing operations, no tactical significance.
Stationary cargo vessel with smaller unknown craft departing at speed without AIS. Possible ship-to-ship transfer or smuggling activity. Worth closer observation while maintaining safe distance.
Tanker directly ahead at close range on same course but slower speed. Navigation hazard requiring attention but no hostile intent. Prepare to overtake safely.
Fast military contact on intercept heading without AIS at dusk. Speed and profile suggest patrol craft or fast attack vessel. Alert command while maintaining situational awareness.
Small fishing fleet operating at distance with all transponders active. Coordinated movement consistent with trawling operations in known fishing area.
Unknown low-profile contact closing rapidly from astern in poor conditions without identification. Overtaking approach at night with degraded comms suggests shadowing or intercept attempt.
Two commercial vessels on crossing courses ahead, both with active AIS. Standard traffic separation scenario, will pass clear with current headings.
Stationary recreational vessel directly on our track. Likely anchored or adrift. No AIS is common for small pleasure craft. Alter course slightly to pass safely while observing.

Two high-speed unknown contacts converging from flanking positions while a third vessel holds position ahead. Coordinated intercept pattern with comms denial indicates deliberate hostile action. Abort mission immediately.
Military vessel on crossing course with active AIS in good conditions. Likely allied or neutral naval unit conducting routine operations. Maintain awareness but no immediate concern.
